title: mentha aquatica var. rauscheri
intro: Mentha aquatica var. rauscheri, also known as Rausch's watermint, is a variety of watermint that is commonly found in wetlands and streams. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to aid digestion and relieve nausea, due to its high content of menthol and other volatile oils. The health benefits of Rausch's watermint include its ability to alleviate symptoms of indigestion, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Rausch's watermint include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote digestive health and relieve respiratory issues.
